message.content: Adversaries may exploit network logon scripts, which are automatically executed at logon initialization, to establish persistence within a network. These scripts can be assigned via Active Directory or Group Policy Objects and run with the privileges of the assigned user. This technique can impact multiple or all systems within a network, depending on the configuration. Common exploitation methods include embedding malicious commands or scripts within these logon scripts to maintain access and execute unauthorized actions. The potential impact includes unauthorized access, data exfiltration, and system compromise. Mitigation strategies involve restricting write access to logon scripts, regularly auditing and monitoring script changes, and employing least privilege principles to limit the impact of compromised accounts. Additionally, implementing robust logging and alerting mechanisms can help detect and respond to suspicious activities related to logon scripts.
